meaning: n.眼花缭乱,喧闹,旋转木马
examples: He razzle-dazzled girls in basket ball.
He returned to Broadway almost three decades later, filling in as the razzle-dazzle lawyer Billy Flynn in "Chicago" in 2003.
For all its flat-screen razzle and high-bandwidth dazzle, it seems the world has run out of major ideas.
Enjoyment and disbelief coexist quite happily in the razzle-dazzle of the early reels.
